name: ESXi Host Disconnected from vCenter
description: A nested ESXi host shows as disconnected in vCenter. Root cause is DNS resolution failing for the host FQDN.
customerScenario: We've got a warning in vCenter saying one of our hosts is disconnected. The VMs on it seem to still be running but we can't manage it through vCenter. What's going on?
customerFollowUp: We did make some changes to our DNS server earlier this week. Could that be related?
fixSteps: Check DNS A record for host FQDN
Check DNS PTR record for host IP
hints: Start by trying to reconnect the host in vCenter. Does it succeed or fail with an error message?
Check whether you can ping the host by IP address and by FQDN from the vCenter server. Is there a difference?
Try resolving the host's FQDN from the vCenter server using nslookup or Resolve-DnsName. Does it return the correct IP?
Check the DNS server for the host's A record and PTR record. One or both may be missing or pointing to the wrong IP after the DNS changes.
Add or correct the A record and PTR record for the host in DNS. Then right-click the host in vCenter and select Connect. It should reconnect successfully.
learningObjectives: Correlate a vCenter host disconnection with DNS misconfiguration
Test ESXi host forward and reverse DNS resolution from vCenter
Correct missing or incorrect A and PTR records on a DNS server
Reconnect a disconnected ESXi host in vCenter after DNS is repaired
author: Jon — CloudITBlog.com
